findings.text: The grain did not finish the work. It prepared the body for it.
findings.deletionTest: It prepared the body for it.
findings.reason: The affirmative second sentence already carries the mechanism; the negated first half is removable scaffolding.
findings.suggestedFix: The grain prepared the body for it.

findings.text: He turned the route diagram once and followed the lines the way commanders followed roads, not as marks on parchment but as movement, distance, supply.
findings.deletionTest: He turned the route diagram once and followed the lines the way commanders followed roads, in terms of movement, distance, and supply.
findings.reason: The comparison lands cleanly without the negated half; the pivot delays an image the sentence can state directly.
findings.suggestedFix: He turned the route diagram once and followed the lines the way commanders followed roads, in terms of movement, distance, and supply.

findings.text: "Your Highness." Sylvanas stood two steps back and to the right, already arranged for the room before the room finished arranging itself around her.
findings.subject: the room
findings.realAgent: court protocol and the people around Arthas taking their places
findings.reason: The room is given self-organizing agency that belongs to protocol and the people physically positioning themselves in it.
findings.suggestedFix: "Your Highness." Sylvanas stood two steps back and to the right, already where protocol would put her before the others had fully settled.

findings.text: Cyndia's gelding gave two side-tilting steps that argued hard for turning back.
findings.subject: two side-tilting steps
findings.realAgent: the gelding's sidling movement making its reluctance visible
findings.reason: The steps are made to argue instead of the prose naming the horse's visible attempt to turn away.
findings.suggestedFix: Cyndia's gelding gave two side-tilting steps toward the shoulder, trying hard to turn back.

findings.text: It looked alive: gates open, traffic moving, wagons unloading and leaving empty for more.
findings.reason: This is the fifth narration colon in the chapter and the weakest over-cap instance; the generic predicate can be dropped and the inventory can be stated directly.
findings.suggestedFix: Gates stood open, traffic moved, wagons unloaded and left empty for more.

summary.verdictReason: Fail: 2 moderate intent-smear kills remain (the room arranging itself; the steps arguing for retreat) plus 3 mild mechanical kills. Rhythm also stays structurally flat in several audited zones.

notes: structural rhythm concerns: comma/period ratio is well below target
structural rhythm concerns: sentence-length variance is slightly above the allowed ceiling
structural rhythm concerns: one-sentence paragraph rate is far above target

textureMetrics.interpretation: PARTICIPIAL PHRASES: 1.8% (floor: 5.0%, human: 11.0%). Add ', Ving' phrases to connect actions: ', turning back to the fire' / ', gripping the railing with both hands'. These create connective tissue between actions.
